sections.name: Second Spawn

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 85
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 85

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

sections.text: The timber body of a second spawn cracks with each movement, as if its very frame protests its abominable mobility. Grown from arboreals native to the Plane of Wood, the greatest of the second spawn—in effect, the “First Spawn”—is Treerazer's mobile fortress, the Witchbole.

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 85
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 85

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

family.name: Cliftwood Fiend

sections.name: Demonic Elementals

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 84
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 84

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

sections.text: Cleftwood fiends aren't true demons or elementals, but they possess both of those creature traits. Since they aren't formed from sinful souls, they lack the typical sin vulnerability most demons possess, and since they possess blood-like sap, they lack the typical immunity to bleed most elementals possess.

sections.name: Other Cleftwood Fiends

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 84
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 84

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

sections.text: Rift pulpers and second spawn are the only types of cleftwood fiend currently active in Tanglebriar, but others exist in the Outer Rifts, particularly in the realm of Jeharlu. Here, one might encounter the blooddrinking stump-like splintertooth, the humanoid primal-magic-wielding blighted gardener, or the enormous horror of the fell deadfall.

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 84
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 84

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

family.text: Cleftwood fiends blend the properties of demons and wood elementals, their timber bodies distorted by burls, rot, and fungal growths. Planar scholars have debated into which category cleftwood fiends are better categorized, but to cleftwoods themselves there is no question—they feel much more at home in the Outer Rifts than they do the Plane of Wood.

traits.name: Rare
traits.text: This rarity indicates that a rules element is very difficult to find in the game world. A rare feat, spell, item or the like is available to players only if the GM decides to include it in the game, typically through discovery during play. Creatures with this trait are rare. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creatures is increased by 5.

traits.name: Demon
traits.text: A family of sinful fiends from the Outer Rifts.

traits.name: Elemental
traits.text: Elementals are creatures directly tied to an element and native to the elemental planes. Elementals don’t need to breathe.

traits.name: Fiend
traits.text: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the unholy planes are called fiends. Fiends can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

traits.name: Wood
traits.text:
Effects with the wood trait conjure or manipulate wood. Those that manipulate wood have no effect in an area without wood. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of wood or have a connection to magical wood.  
  
These planes consist of trees and other flora latticed in organic patterns. Wood planes are often perfectly constructed to match their desired purpose or environment but aren’t usually outright hostile to visitors.

automatic_abilities.effect: A spray of bloody sap spurts from the second spawn's wound, targeting a creature within 30 feet of the second spawn's choice. That creature takes 10d6 poison damage (DC 41 basic Fortitude save) and is exposed to corrupting sap.
automatic_abilities.name: Spray Sap

traits.name: Reach
traits.text: This weapon can be used to attack enemies up to 10 feet away instead of only adjacent enemies. For creatures with reach, the weapon increases their reach by 5 feet.

traits.name: Brutal
traits.text: A ranged attack with this trait uses its Strength modifier instead of Dexterity on the attack roll.

traits.name: Propulsive
traits.text: You add half your Strength modifier (if positive) to damage rolls with a propulsive ranged weapon. If you have a negative Strength modifier, you add your full Strength modifier instead.

traits.name: Range
traits.text: These attacks will either list a finite range or a range increment, which follows the normal rules for range increments.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

ability.critical_failure: The creature is controlled by the second spawn for 1 minute.
ability.critical_success: The creature is unaffected, and is temporarily immune to corrupting sap for 1 minute.
ability.failure: The creature is confused for 1 minute.
ability.name: Corrupting Sap
ability.success: Intrusive fiendish thoughts and cruel urges well up in the creature's mind, causing the creature to become off-guard until the end of its next turn.
ability.text: The first time in a round a creature is exposed to the second spawn's corrupting sap, the creature must attempt a DC 44 Will save.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Incapacitation
traits.text: An ability with this trait can take a character completely out of the fight or even kill them, and it’s harder to use on a more powerful character. If a spell has the incapacitation trait, any creature of more than twice the spell’s rank treats the result of their check to prevent being incapacitated by the spell as one degree of success better, or the result of any check the spellcaster made to incapacitate them as one degree of success worse. If any other effect has the incapacitation trait, a creature of higher level than the item, creature, or hazard generating the effect gains the same benefits.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

universal_monster_ability.name: Darkvision
universal_monster_ability.text: A monster with darkvision can see perfectly well in areas of darkness and dim light, though such vision is in black and white only. Some forms of magical darkness, such as a 4th-level *darkness* spell, block normal darkvision. A monster with greater darkvision, however, can see through even these forms of magical darkness.

link.alt: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 85
link.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight pg. 85

sources.name: Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight

universal_monster_ability.name: Telepathy
universal_monster_ability.text: A monster with telepathy can communicate mentally with any creatures within the listed radius, as long as they share a language. This doesn't give any special access to their thoughts and communicates no more information than normal speech would.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.
